narrative.overview: Bantaba is the Mandinka word for the gathering place under the village meeting tree, and the interface holds that posture: a quiet, dark room where a team's work — humans and AI agents together — gathers and stays legible. The ground is a near-black teal, surfaces step up through four close-set tonal layers, and a single emerald voice marks what is actively alive. This is a product surface: design serves the task, with earned familiarity executed in terminal-adjacent confidence — mono identifiers, hexagonal marks, truthful status language.
keyCharacteristics: Dark teal-black tonal layering; depth by surface steps and 1px borders, not shadows
One emerald accent reserved for actions, selection, and live/healthy state
Truthful status hues: amber = degraded, red = failed, blue = waiting — always dot + label
System sans at a fixed px scale; mono strictly for machine truth
Hexagons as the single brand motif
Motion is minimal and state-bound; WCAG 2.1 AA is the enforced floor

rules.name: The One Emerald Voice Rule
rules.body: The accent appears on at most ~10% of any screen: primary actions, current selection, live/healthy state. Emerald as decoration is prohibited.

rules.name: The Truthful Hue Rule
rules.body: A status color may only render a state the signed event log proves. Status is never conveyed by color alone — always a dot (or glyph) plus a text label.

rules.name: The Quiet Mono Rule
rules.body: Mono marks machine truth only — ids, addresses, error codes, commands, progress numbers. Mono headings or decorative mono are prohibited.

rules.name: The Border-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces separate by tonal step and 1px border. If a new component seems to need a shadow, it's either a modal or it's wrong.

dos: Do keep the emerald voice under ~10% of any screen — actions, selection, live state only.
Do pair every status color with a dot/glyph and a text label; render only states the event log proves.
Do hold the AA floor: ≥4.5:1 for body and small info-bearing text on its actual surface — recede via muted grounds and dimmed marks, never blanket opacity over text.
Do ship every interactive state: default, hover, focus-visible, active, disabled — plus honest loading skeletons and empty states that teach.
Do use the token system (--*-dim / --*-line companions, the z-index scale) instead of raw rgba/z literals.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion for any animation; keep transitions in the 150–250ms band, easing out.
donts: Don't produce generic SaaS dashboard slop: cream/light defaults, purple-blue gradients, identical KPI card grids, hero-metric tiles.
Don't build fake-state UI: optimistic delivered checks, spinners implying progress that isn't happening, silent partial fetches.
Don't drift toward the crypto/web3 aesthetic: glow-everything, gradient text, neon hexagons — the HexMark logo is the only gradient and dots the only glow.
Don't skin it like a chat app: agents, files, and pipes are first-class panes, not bolted-on integrations.
Don't nest bordered cards. One card level; inner tiles sit on the nested surface inside flat rows.
Don't use side-stripe borders, decorative glassmorphism, or display/mono fonts in UI labels.
Don't let unbroken tokens (64-hex ids, URLs, paths) overflow: every text surface carries truncation or overflow-wrap.
